aboutPage.heading:
Tullio
Philippe
aboutPage.intro: Born in Paris, Philippe Tullio discovered his passion for filmmaking through acting — a dual training that shaped a singular approach to direction.
aboutPage.actingBody: He moved to Los Angeles to study the Meisner and Stanislavski methods under Joanne Baron, one of Hollywood's most respected acting coaches — whose clients include Halle Berry and Leslie Mann. This immersion in performance shaped an intimate understanding of actor direction.
aboutPage.filmschoolBody1: Tullio's passion for film led him to join the London Film Academy in 2007, where he honed his skills in directing.
aboutPage.filmschoolBody2: At the Academy, he met Lincoln Ascott, a teacher who inspired him and convinced him to work as an actor and assistant director on three short films.
aboutPage.kcitizenBlock: At the age of 25, Tullio produced and directed his first medium-length film, "K-CITIZEN," which tackled the excesses of the American dream. He followed this with a satirical comedy titled "Jesus is Back and He's Not Happy."
aboutPage.scarfaceDesc: A drama based on real-life events, which tells the story of an Afghan multimillionaire living in Los Angeles who squanders his 8-figure fortune in just six months.
aboutPage.jayaDesc: An action film co-written with Jeremy Minuit. Teaser and mood board available.
aboutPage.crakoDesc: A horror and parkour movie featuring David Belle, the founder of parkour. Currently in development.
aboutPage.covidDesc: During the COVID-19 lockdown, Tullio wrote "OFF-SIDE," an offbeat series about a modern soccer anti-hero.
aboutPage.commercialDesc: Tullio produced and shot a commercial for the fashion brand ABACO Paris, featuring actor and ex-professional boxer Stéphane Ferrara.
